1
18#ifndef MEDIA_C_LIST_H_
19#define MEDIA_C_LIST_H_
20
21#include <stdbool.h>
22#include <stdio.h>
23#include <stdlib.h>
24#include <string.h>
25
26#include "error.h"
27#include "value.h"
28
29#ifdef __cplusplus
30extern "C" {
31#endif
32
44typedef struct {
45 void* items;
46 size_t size;
47 size_t capacity;
48 size_t item_size;
49} list_t;
50
58static inline list_t* list_new(size_t initial_capacity, size_t item_size) {
59 list_t* list = (list_t*)malloc(sizeof(list_t));
60 if (list != NULL) {
61 list->items = malloc(initial_capacity * item_size);
62 if (list->items == NULL) {
63 free(list);
64 list = NULL;
65 } else {
66 list->size = 0;
67 list->capacity = initial_capacity;
68 list->item_size = item_size;
69 }
70 }
71 return list;
72}
73
80static inline void list_clear(list_t* list, void (*free_func)(void*)) {
81 if ((list != NULL) && (free_func != NULL)) {
82 char* base = (char*)list->items; /* cppcheck-suppress misra-c2012-11.5 ; byte-level access to void* buffer */
83 for (size_t i = 0; i < list->size; ++i) {
84 free_func(&base[i * list->item_size]);
85 }
86 list->size = 0;
87 }
88}
89
96static inline void list_free(list_t* list, void (*free_func)(void*)) {
97 if (list != NULL) {
98 list_clear(list, free_func);
99 free(list->items);
100 free(list);
101 }
102}
103
111static inline bool list_add(list_t* list, void* item) {
112 bool result = true;
113 if (list->size >= list->capacity) {
114 size_t new_capacity = list->capacity * 2U;
115 void* new_items = realloc(list->items, new_capacity * list->item_size);
116 if (new_items == NULL) {
117 result = false;
118 } else {
119 list->items = new_items;
120 list->capacity = new_capacity;
121 }
122 }
123 if (result) {
124 (void)memcpy(&((char*)list->items)[list->size * list->item_size], item, list->item_size);
125 list->size++;
126 }
127 return result;
128}
129
137static inline void* list_get(const list_t* list, size_t index) {
138 void* result = NULL;
139 if (index < list->size) {
140 result = &((char*)list->items)[index * list->item_size];
141 }
142 return result;
143}
144
153static inline bool list_set(const list_t* list, size_t index, void* item) {
154 bool result = false;
155 if (index < list->size) {
156 (void)memcpy(&((char*)list->items)[index * list->item_size], item, list->item_size);
157 result = true;
158 }
159 return result;
160}
161
170static inline bool list_remove(list_t* list, size_t index, void (*free_func)(void*)) {
171 bool result = false;
172 if (index < list->size) {
173 if (free_func != NULL) {
174 free_func(&((char*)list->items)[index * list->item_size]);
175 }
176 for (size_t i = index; i < (list->size - 1U); ++i) {
177 (void)memcpy(&((char*)list->items)[i * list->item_size], &((char*)list->items)[(i + 1U) * list->item_size],
178 list->item_size);
179 }
180 list->size--;
181 result = true;
182 }
183 return result;
184}
185
192static inline size_t list_size(const list_t* list) { return list->size; }
193
200static inline size_t list_capacity(const list_t* list) { return list->capacity; }
201
214static inline int list_check_valid(const list_t* list, size_t expected_item_size, bool must_be_non_empty) {
215 int ret;
216 if (list == NULL) {
217 ret = MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETER;
218 } else if ((list->item_size == 0U) || (list->capacity == 0U)) {
219 ret = MEDIA_ERROR_NOT_FOUND;
220 } else if (list->item_size != expected_item_size) {
221 ret = MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTED;
222 } else if (list->size > list->capacity) {
223 ret = MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTED;
224 } else if ((list->items == NULL) && (list->size > 0U)) {
225 ret = MEDIA_ERROR_NOT_FOUND;
226 } else if (must_be_non_empty && (list->size == 0U)) {
227 ret = MEDIA_ERROR_NOT_FOUND;
228 } else {
229 ret = MEDIA_ERROR_NONE;
230 }
231 return ret;
232}
233
234#ifdef __cplusplus
235}
236#endif
237
238#endif // MEDIA_C_LIST_H_
error.h
Error codes and error information structures for the Media C API.
list_check_valid
static int list_check_valid(const list_t *list, size_t expected_item_size, bool must_be_non_empty)
Validate a list_t instance for basic integrity.

Free the list and all of its items.

Remove an item at the given index, shifting subsequent items.
